poster_title: BLIP: Bootstrapping Language-Image Pre-training for Unified Vision-Language Understanding and Generation
authors: Junnan Li, Dongxu Li, Caiming Xiong, Steven Hoi

content: VLP models excel in either understanding or generation, not both.
Performance gains from noisy web data are significant but overlooked.
BLIP aims to bridge the gap between understanding and generation tasks.
Proposes a new dataset bootstrapping method (CapFilt).
Introduces a multimodal mixture of encoder-decoder (MED) architecture.
Encoder-based models struggle with text generation tasks.
Encoder-decoder models are not suitable for image-text retrieval.
Noisy web data negatively impacts VLP performance.
CapFilt addresses the issue of noisy data in VLP.
MED architecture enables effective multi-task pre-training.
MED model operates as unimodal encoder, image-grounded text encoder, or image-grounded text decoder.
CapFilt generates synthetic captions and filters noisy web text.
BLIP pre-trains on large-scale noisy web data using MED and CapFilt.
Model architecture and objectives are illustrated in Fig5.
Evaluated on image-text retrieval, image captioning, VQA, visual reasoning, and visual dialog.
Zero-shot video-language tasks include text-to-video retrieval and videoQA.
Comprehensive experiments demonstrate BLIP's versatility and robustness.
State-of-the-art results in image-text retrieval (+2.7% in average recall@1).
Improvement in image captioning (+2.8% in CIDEr).
Enhanced VQA performance (+1.6% in VQA score).
Examples of web text and synthetic text are shown in Fig6.
BLIP effectively leverages noisy web data for VLP.
CapFilt significantly improves data quality.
MED architecture enables flexible transfer learning.
BLIP shows strong generalization in zero-shot video-language tasks.
BLIP achieves state-of-the-art performance in vision-language tasks.
CapFilt and MED offer a powerful approach to VLP.
Future work will explore the effectiveness in other domains.

Fig5.caption: Pre-training model architecture and objectives of BLIP (same parameters have the same color).
Fig5.description: 展示了 BLIP 预训练模型的架构和目标，不同参数用不同颜色表示

Fig6.caption: Examples of the web text Tw and the synthetic text Ts. Green texts are accepted by the filter, whereas red texts are rejected.
Fig6.description: 展示了 web 文本和合成文本的示例，绿色文本被过滤器接受，红色文本被拒绝
